script_text: I had a client who wanted to put a lot of their portfolio in one tech stock, Mostly because they worked in that industry and felt confident about it. I recommended more diversification… based on their risk tolerance. But they pushed back,um…, because they felt they had good insight.Instead of just agreeing uh.., or arguing, I asked them to walk me through their thinking, so I could understand it better. Then we talked through some scenario…,like, for example, what would happen if that stock dropped? I acknowledged that they knew the industry well but explained diversification was really about managing risk. We ended up compromising…., they kept a bigger position than I suggested, but not as concentrated as they wanted. And, they appreciated that I listened.

script_text: I had a client who wanted to put a lot of their portfolio in one tech stock because they worked in that industry and felt confident about it. I recommended more diversification based on their risk tolerance. They pushed back because they felt they had good insight. Instead of just agreeing or arguing, I asked them to walk me through their thinking so I could understand it better. Then we talked through some scenarios—like what would happen if that stock dropped. I acknowledged they knew the industry well, but explained diversification was about managing risk. We ended up compromising—they kept a bigger position than I suggested but not as concentrated as they wanted. They appreciated that I listened.

script_text: I had a client who wanted to open a retirement account But, uh... didn't really understand the difference between a traditional IRA and a Roth IRA. They ended up opening a Roth and told me later they appreciated that I explained it clearly without pushing one option.

Instead of just agreeing or arguing, I asked them to walk me through their thinking so I could understand it better. Then we talked through some scenarios—like what would happen if that stock dropped. I acknowledged they knew the industry well, but explained diversification was about managing risk. We ended up compromising—they kept a bigger position than I suggested but not as concentrated as they wanted. They appreciated that I listened.

script_text: I had a client who wanted to open a retirement account But, uh... didn't really understand the difference between a traditional IRA and a Roth IRA. I used a simple example… with actual numbers just to show how each might work for them. And, actually, I made sure to mention I wasn't giving tax advice... just explaining the options. They ended up opening a Roth and told me later they appreciated that I explained it clearly without pushing one option.

I apologized and we rescheduled the meeting to Monday. The client appreciated the heads-up, and I got the report to them Friday afternoon. I learned it's way better to communicate early when priorities shift rather than just missing a deadline.

script_text: I had a client who wanted to put a lot of their portfolio in one tech stock because they worked in that industry and felt confident about it. I recommended more diversification based on their risk tolerance. They pushed back because they felt they had good insight. Instead of just agreeing or arguing, I asked them to walk me through their thinking so I could understand it better. Then we talked through some scenarios—like what would happen if that stock dropped. I acknowledged they knew the industry well, but explained diversification was about managing risk. We ended up compromising—they kept a bigger position than I suggested but not as concentrated as they wanted. They appreciated that I listened.

script_text: I had a client who wanted to open a retirement account But, uh... didn't really understand the difference between a traditional IRA and a Roth IRA. So, I asked them some questions about their tax situation and timeline first, and then explained it as basically 'pay taxes now or pay taxes later. I used a simple example… with actual numbers just to show how each might work for them. And, actually, I made sure to mention I wasn't giving tax advice... just explaining the options. They had more questions, so, um... I walked through a comparison and suggested they talk to a tax professional too. They ended up opening a Roth and told me later they appreciated that I explained it clearly without pushing one option.
